title: Software
text:
I develop and maintain R packages for econometrics, with a focus on discrete choice models, limited dependent variable models, and spatial econometrics.


My software is designed to make advanced econometric models usable for applied researchers, with emphasis on transparent syntax, simulation-based estimation, marginal effects, and reproducible empirical work.


GitHub CRAN packages Research







Discrete choice Mixed logit Latent class Random parameters Conditional logit
gmnl estimates multinomial logit models with observed and unobserved preference heterogeneity. It includes mixed multinomial logit, generalized multinomial logit, latent class, and mixed-mixed logit models.

CRAN · GitHub · JSS article · Manual

install.packages("gmnl")



Limited dependent variables Random parameters Binary models Ordered models Count models
Rchoice estimates binary, ordered, and count models with random parameters using simulated maximum likelihood for cross-sectional and longitudinal data.

CRAN · GitHub · JSS article · Manual

install.packages("Rchoice")



Spatial econometrics Binary response models GMM Marginal effects Maximum likelihood
spldv estimates spatial limited dependent variable models, especially spatial autoregressive models for binary outcomes using GMM estimators.

CRAN · GitHub · Article · Manual

install.packages("spldv")



Spatial econometrics Spatial regimes Heterogeneity Endogeneity
hspm implements heterogeneous spatial models, including spatial regimes models with spatial lags, spatially lagged regressors, endogenous variables, and spatially autocorrelated errors.

CRAN · GitHub · Manual

install.packages("hspm")








Package
Main area
Main contribution




gmnl
Discrete choice
Multinomial logit models with random parameters, latent classes, and flexible heterogeneity.


Rchoice
Limited dependent variables
Binary, ordered, and count models with random parameters.


spldv
Spatial econometrics
Spatial binary response models estimated by GMM.


hspm
Spatial heterogeneity
Spatial regimes and heterogeneous spatial models.






I also use GitHub to host research code, replication material, and work-in-progress implementations related to spatial econometrics, discrete choice, and limited dependent variable models.

View my GitHub profile

title: Mauricio Sarrias
text:
Associate Professor of Economics
Facultad de Economía y Negocios, Universidad de Talca, Chile
I am a regional scientist (pseudo economist) working on spatial econometrics, discrete choice models, and binary response models. I develop econometric methods and R software for researchers working with spatial dependence, heterogeneous preferences, and limited dependent variables.

Research Download CV






Research areas
Spatial econometrics Discrete choice Random parameters Causal inference R packages Simulation
Quick links

My web of science
My researchgate
My google scholar
My scopus
My github








I study econometric models for spatially dependent data, discrete choices, binary outcomes, endogenous regressors, and heterogeneous effects.
See research



I develop and maintain R tools for applied econometrics, including work related to multinomial logit models and spatial limited dependent variable models.
See software



I teach econometrics, causal inference, discrete choice models, and spatial econometrics at undergraduate, master, and doctoral levels.
See teaching

title: Teaching
text:
I teach courses in econometrics, spatial econometrics, discrete choice models, microeconomics, and computational methods for applied economic research. Most of my teaching materials emphasize the connection between econometric theory, empirical applications, and implementation in R.


3
Courses and workshops


2
Graduate courses


0
Workshops




Causal inference Consumer theory Cross-section Econometrics GeoDa IV/GMM Maximum likelihood Microeconomics Panel data R Spatial econometrics Specification testing Stata Theory of the firm Welfare analysis










Graduate · Universidad Católica del Norte



March–July 2018; updated teaching materials in 2020



Econometric methods and applications for cross-section and panel data, with emphasis on estimation beyond classical OLS, applied work, and computational implementation.


Software: R, Stata

Econometrics Cross-section Panel data Causal inference R Stata


📄Syllabus 🖥️Class notes and slides ▶️Video: Law of iterated expectations







Graduate · Universidad de Talca



October–December 2020



Introduction to the methodology and application of spatial econometric models for Master and PhD students, including model specification, testing, maximum likelihood, IV/GMM estimation, and applied work in R and GeoDa.


Software: R, GeoDa

Spatial econometrics Maximum likelihood IV/GMM Specification testing R GeoDa


📄Syllabus 🖥️Class notes and materials 📘Spatial Econometrics book/notes ▶️Videos in Spanish 🧪Labs and homeworks












Undergraduate · Universidad de Talca



March–June 2023



Microeconomic theory course covering consumer theory, utility, the consumer problem, comparative statics, welfare analysis, and theory of the firm.

Microeconomics Consumer theory Welfare analysis Theory of the firm


🖥️Slides and handouts ✏️Homeworks 📝Previous exams

title: Research
text: My research focuses on spatial econometrics, discrete choice models, binary response models, simulation-based estimation, causal inference, and applied microeconometrics.

title: Research
text:
Publications

A J-test for spatial autoregressive binary models
Gianfranco Piras and Mauricio Sarrias. Spatial Statistics, 2025.
Spatial econometrics Binary response models Discrete choice Simulation
Develops a J-test for spatial autoregressive binary response models and evaluates its finite-sample performance through simulation and applied evidence.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Happy Where You Are? Exploring Migration Intentions and Subjective Well-being Through a Discrete Choice Experiment
Victor Iturra and Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Happiness Studies, 2025.
Discrete choice Subjective well-being Latent class Random parameters
Studies how subjective well-being shapes migration intentions using a discrete choice experiment that captures heterogeneity in location preferences.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Instrumental variable estimation with observed and unobserved heterogeneity of the treatment and instrument effect: a latent class approach
Pablo Rodriguez and Mauricio Sarrias. Empirical Economics, 2024.
Discrete choice Causal inference Latent class
Develops a latent-class IV framework for treatment-effect estimation when both treatment responses and instrument effects are heterogeneous.
DOI · Code · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Amenities and individual heterogeneity in the return to schooling
Victor Iturra and Mauricio Sarrias. Letters in Spatial and Resource Sciences, 2023.
Discrete choice Education Random parameters Amenities
Analyzes how local amenities and individual heterogeneity shape the estimated returns to schooling.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Estimating Heteroskedastic and Instrumental Variable Models for Binary Outcome Variables in R
Mauricio Sarrias. The R Journal, 2023.
Binary response models Discrete choice Causal inference R package
Introduces R tools for binary outcome models with heteroskedasticity and endogenous regressors, with emphasis on applied microeconometric use.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


GMM Estimators for Binary Spatial Models in R
Gianfranco Piras and Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Statistical Software, 2023.
Spatial econometrics Binary response models Discrete choice models R package
Presents GMM estimators for binary spatial models and their implementation in R, combining methodological detail with reproducible software examples.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Heterogeneous spatial models in R: spatial regimes models
Gianfranco Piras and Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Spatial Econometrics, 2023.
Spatial econometrics R package Random parameters
Provides R tools for spatial regimes models, allowing spatial relationships and covariate effects to differ across groups or regions.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


One or two-step? Evaluating GMM efficiency for spatial binary probit models
Gianfranco Piras and Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Choice Modelling, 2023.
Spatial econometrics Binary response models Simulation
Compares one-step and two-step GMM strategies for spatial binary probit models, focusing on efficiency, bias, and practical estimation trade-offs.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Bodyweight and human capital development: Assessing the impact of obesity on socioemotional skills during childhood in Chile
Mauricio Sarrias and Alejandra Blanco. Economics & Human Biology, 2022.
Causal inference Health economics
Estimates the effect of childhood obesity on socioemotional skill formation in Chile, linking health status to human capital development.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Estimating the non-pecuniary benefit of engaging in pro-environmental behaviors: Incorporating both heterogeneous preferences and income endogeneity
Cristian Ortiz and Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Environmental Management, 2022.
Discrete choice Causal inference Environmental economics Latent class Random parameters
Estimates the non-pecuniary benefits of pro-environmental behavior while accounting for preference heterogeneity and income endogeneity.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


The Impact of Bodyweight on Life Satisfaction among School-Aged Children: Are the Mechanisms Gender-Based?
Victor Iturra and Mauricio Sarrias. Child Indicators Research, 2022.
Causal inference Subjective well-being Health economics
Assesses how bodyweight affects life satisfaction among school-aged children and whether the underlying mechanisms differ by gender.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Valuing urban drinking water supply attributes: A case study from Chile
Cristian González-Santander and Mauricio Sarrias and Ricardo A. Daziano and Lisandro Roco. Water Resources and Economics, 2022.
Discrete choice Environmental economics Mixed logit Random parameters
Uses discrete choice methods to value urban drinking-water attributes in Chile, highlighting household preferences over service quality.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Your Air Pollution Makes Me Sick! Estimating the Spatial Spillover Effects of PM2.5 Emissions on Emergency Room Visits in Chile
Mauricio Sarrias and Anita Molina-Varas. REGION, 2022.
Spatial econometrics Health economics Environmental economics
Estimates spatial spillover effects of PM2.5 emissions on emergency-room visits in Chile, highlighting health impacts beyond local exposure.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


A two recursive equation model to correct for endogeneity in latent class binary probit models
Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Choice Modelling, 2021.
Discrete choice Binary response models Causal inference Latent class Random parameters
Develops a recursive-equation approach to correct endogeneity in latent-class binary probit models.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


The Double Burden of Being A Woman and Obese: Evidence from the Chilean Labor Market
Mauricio Sarrias and Victor Iturra. Feminist Economics, 2021.
Health economics
Studies the labor-market penalties associated with obesity in Chile, emphasizing the additional burden faced by women.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Are preferences for city attributes heterogeneous? An assessment using a discrete choice experiment
Diana Romero-Espinosa and Mauricio Sarrias and Ricardo Daziano. Papers in Regional Science, 2021.
Discrete choice Mixed logit Random parameters Amenities
Uses a discrete choice experiment to measure heterogeneity in preferences for city attributes and urban amenities.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Effects of spatial competition on public educational efficiency: an analysis for the Chilean Metropolitan Region
Camilo Quintero-Fragozo and Yasna Cortés and Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Education Policy, 2021.
Causal inference Education Regional economics
Analyzes how spatial competition among municipalities affects public educational efficiency in the Chilean Metropolitan Region.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Individual-specific posterior distributions from Mixed Logit models: Properties, limitations and diagnostic checks
Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Choice Modelling, 2020.
Discrete choice Simulation Mixed logit Random parameters
Studies individual-specific posterior distributions from mixed logit models, clarifying their properties, limitations, and diagnostic use.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Random Parameters and Spatial Heterogeneity using Rchoice in R
Mauricio Sarrias. REGION, 2020.
Spatial econometrics Discrete choice R package Mixed logit Random parameters
Shows how the Rchoice package can estimate random-parameter models and spatial heterogeneity in applied regional analysis.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


How Much Should We Pay for Mental Health Deterioration? The Subjective Monetary Value of Mental Health After the 27F Chilean Earthquake
Mauricio Sarrias and Benjamin Jara. Journal of Happiness Studies, 2019.
Subjective well-being Health economics
Estimates the subjective monetary value of mental-health deterioration after the 27F Chilean earthquake.
DOI · Code · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


The monetary subjective health evaluation for commuting long distances in Chile: A latent class analysis
J. Palomino and M. Sarrias. Papers in Regional Science, 2019.
Discrete choice Latent class Subjective well-being Health economics Transportation Random parameters
Uses latent-class methods to estimate the subjective health cost of long-distance commuting in Chile.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


We Do Not Have the Same Tastes! Evaluating Individual Heterogeneity in the Preferences for Amenities
G. Ahumada and V. Iturra and M. Sarrias. Journal of Happiness Studies, 2019.
Discrete choice Regional economics Random parameters Mixed logit Amenities
Evaluates individual heterogeneity in preferences for amenities, showing that willingness to pay varies substantially across people.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Do monetary subjective well-being evaluations vary across space? Comparing continuous and discrete spatial heterogeneity
Mauricio Sarrias. Spatial Economic Analysis, 2018.
Spatial econometrics Subjective well-being Random parameters Mixed logit Latent class
Compares continuous and discrete models of spatial heterogeneity in subjective well-being valuations.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Individual-specific point and interval conditional estimates of latent class logit parameters
Mauricio Sarrias and Ricardo A. Daziano. Journal of Choice Modelling, 2018.
Discrete choice Latent class Random parameters
Derives and applies individual-specific point and interval estimates for parameters in latent-class logit models.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Regional concentration and national economic growth in Brazil and Chile
P. Aroca and C. Azzoni and M. Sarrias. Letters in Spatial and Resource Sciences, 2018.
Regional economics
Examines the relationship between regional concentration and national economic growth in Brazil and Chile.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Are consumers willing to pay to let cars drive for them? Analyzing response to autonomous vehicles
Ricardo A. Daziano and Mauricio Sarrias and Benjamin Leard. Transportation Research Part C: Emerging Technologies, 2017.
Discrete choice Transportation Random parameters Mixed logit
Uses stated-choice data to estimate willingness to pay for autonomous vehicle features and related transportation technologies.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Convergence to the managerial frontier
William F. Maloney and Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Economic Behavior & Organization, 2017.
Applied microeconometrics
Studies convergence toward a managerial frontier, linking firm or management performance to productivity dynamics.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Multinomial Logit Models with Continuous and Discrete Individual Heterogeneity in R: The gmnl Package
Mauricio Sarrias and Ricardo Daziano. Journal of Statistical Software, 2017.
Discrete choice R package Mixed logit Random parameters
Introduces the gmnl package for estimating multinomial logit models with continuous and discrete individual heterogeneity in R.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Discrete Choice Models with Random Parameters in R: The Rchoice Package
Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Statistical Software, 2016.
Discrete choice R package Random parameters
Introduces the Rchoice package for estimating discrete choice models with random parameters in R.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex


Heterogeneity in subjective wellbeing: An application to occupational allocation in Africa
Paolo Falco and William F. Maloney and Bob Rijkers and Mauricio Sarrias. Journal of Economic Behavior & Organization, 2015.
Subjective well-being Discrete choice Random parameters
Analyzes heterogeneity in subjective well-being across occupational allocations in Africa using applied discrete choice methods.
DOI · Journal metrics · OpenAlex

title: Students & Alumni
text:
I have advised graduate students working on applied econometrics, spatial econometrics, discrete choice models, causal inference, environmental economics, health economics, education, and human capital.
